sections.label: Section 171
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ; παλαιὰ παρὰ νεωτέρας μάθω .
sections.translation: How then ? Let my age be taught by your youth .

sections.label: Section 172
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν ὅστις πλὴν ἐμοῦ κείραιτό [*] νιν .
sections.translation: There is no one who could have cut it but myself .

sections.label: Section 173
sections.original: ἐχθροὶ γὰρ οἷς προσῆκε πενθῆσαι τριχί .
sections.translation: Yes , for those who ought to have mourned with a lock of hair are enemies .

sections.label: Section 174
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ὅδ ἐστὶ κάρτ ἰδεῖν ὁμόπτερος .
sections.translation: And further , in appearance it is very much like —

sections.label: Section 175
sections.original: ποίαις ἐθείραις ; τοῦτο γὰρ θέλω μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: Whose lock ? This is what I would like to know .

sections.label: Section 176
sections.original: αὐτοῖσιν ἡμῖν κάρτα προσφερὴς ἰδεῖν .
sections.translation: It is very much like my own in appearance .

sections.label: Section 177
sections.original: μῶν οὖν Ὀρέστου κρύβδα δῶρον ἦν [*] τόδε ;
sections.translation: Then can this be a secret offering from Orestes ?

sections.label: Section 178
sections.original: μάλιστ ἐκείνου βοστρύχοις προσείδεται .
sections.translation: It is his curling locks that it most resembles .

sections.label: Section 179
sections.original: καὶ πῶς ἐκεῖνος δεῦρ ἐτόλμησεν μολεῖν ;
sections.translation: But how did he dare to come here ?

sections.label: Section 180
sections.original: ἔπεμψε χαίτην [*] κουρίμην χάριν πατρός .
sections.translation: He has merely sent this cut lock to honor his father .

sections.label: Section 181
sections.original: οὐχ ἧσσον εὐδάκρυτά μοι λέγεις τάδε ,
sections.translation: What you say is no less a cause of tears for me , if he will never again set foot on this land .

sections.label: Section 183
sections.original: κἀμοὶ προσέστη καρδίας κλυδώνιον
sections.translation: Over my heart , too , there sweeps a surge of bitterness , and I am struck as if a sword had run me through .

sections.label: Section 185
sections.original: ἐξ ὀμμάτων δὲ δίψιοι πίπτουσί μοι
sections.translation: From my eyes thirsty drops of a stormy flood fall unchecked at the sight of this tress . For how can I expect to find that someone else , some townsman , owns this lock ? Nor yet in truth did she clip it from her head , the murderess ,

sections.label: Section 190
sections.original: ἐμὴ δὲ μήτηρ , οὐδαμῶς ἐπώνυμον
sections.translation: my own mother , who has assumed a godless spirit regarding her children that ill accords with the name of mother . But as for me , how am I to assent to this outright , that it adorned the head of Orestes , the dearest to me of all mortals ? No , hope is merely flattering me .

sections.label: Section 195
sections.original: εἴθ εἶχε φωνὴν εὔφρον ἀγγέλου δίκην ,
sections.translation: Ah , woe ! If only , like a messenger , it had a kind voice , so that I would not be tossed by my distracted thoughts . Rather it would plainly bid me to spurn this tress , if it was severed from a hated head . Or if it were a kinsman ’ s , he would share my grief

sections.label: Section 200
sections.original: ἄγαλμα τύμβου τοῦδε καὶ τιμὴν πατρός .
sections.translation:
as an adornment to this tomb and a tribute to my father .
But I invoke the gods , who know by what storms we are tossed like seafarers . Yet if I am fated to reach safety , a great stock may come from a little seed .

sections.label: Section 205
sections.original: καὶ μὴν στίβοι γε , δεύτερον τεκμήριον ,
sections.translation: And look ! Another proof ! Footprints matching each other — and like my own ! Yes , here are the outlines of two sets of feet , his own and some companion ’ s . The heels and the imprints of the tendons agree

sections.label: Section 210
sections.original: εἰς ταὐτὸ συμβαίνουσι τοῖς ἐμοῖς στίβοις .
sections.translation: in proportion with my own tracks . I am in torment , my brain is in a whirl !

sections.label: Section 212
sections.original: εὔχου τὰ λοιπά , τοῖς θεοῖς τελεσφόρους
sections.translation: Give recognition to the gods that your prayers have been fulfilled , and pray that success may attend you in the future .

sections.label: Section 214
sections.original: ἐπεὶ τί νῦν ἕκατι δαιμόνων κυρῶ ;
sections.translation: What ? Have I succeeded now by the will of the gods ?

sections.label: Section 215
sections.original: εἰς ὄψιν ἥκεις ὧνπερ ἐξηύχου [*] πάλαι .
sections.translation: You have come to the sight of what you have long prayed for .

sections.label: Section 216
sections.original: καὶ τίνα σύνοισθά μοι καλουμένῃ βροτῶν ;
sections.translation: And do you know whom among mortals I was invoking ?

sections.label: Section 217
sections.original: σύνοιδ Ὀρέστην πολλά σ ἐκπαγλουμένην . [*]
sections.translation: I know that you are pining for Orestes .

sections.label: Section 218
sections.original: καὶ πρὸς τί δῆτα τυγχάνω κατευγμάτων ;
sections.translation: Then how have I found an answer to my prayers ?

sections.label: Section 219
sections.original: ὅδ εἰμί : μὴ μάτευ [*] ἐμοῦ μᾶλλον φίλον .
sections.translation: Here I am . Search for no other friend than me .

sections.label: Section 220
sections.original: ἀλλ ἦ δόλον τιν , ὦ ξέν , ἀμφί μοι πλέκεις ;
sections.translation: But surely , stranger , you are weaving some snare about me ?

sections.label: Section 221
sections.original: αὐτὸς καθ αὑτοῦ τἄρα [*] μηχανορραφῶ .
sections.translation: Then I am devising plots against myself .

sections.label: Section 222
sections.original: ἀλλ ἐν κακοῖσι τοῖς ἐμοῖς γελᾶν θέλεις .
sections.translation: No , you wish to mock my distress .

sections.label: Section 223
sections.original: κἀν τοῖς ἐμοῖς ἄρ , [*] εἴπερ ἔν γε τοῖσι σοῖς .
sections.translation: Then my own also , if yours .

sections.label: Section 224
sections.original: ὡς ὄντ Ὀρέστην τἄρ [*] ἐγὼ προσεννέπω ;
sections.translation: Am I then to address you as Orestes in truth ?

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: αὐτὸν μὲν οὖν [*] ὁρῶσα δυσμαθεῖς ἐμέ :
sections.translation: Now , even though you see him in me , you are slow to learn . Yet at the sight of this tress cut in mourning , and when you were scrutinizing the footprints of my tracks , your thought took wings and you knew you had found me .

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: σκέψαι τομῇ [*] προσθεῖσα βόστρυχον τριχὸς
sections.translation: Put the lock of hair , your own brother ’ s , in the spot it was cut from and observe how it matches the hair on my head . And see this piece of weaving , your handiwork , the strokes of the batten and the beasts in the design . Control yourself ! Do not go mad with joy ! For I know that our nearest kin are bitter foes to us both .

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατον μέλημα δώμασιν πατρός ,
sections.translation: O best beloved darling of your father ’ s house , its hope of a saving seed longed for with tears , trust in your prowess and you will win back your father ’ s house . O delightful eyes that have four parts of love for me : for I must call you father ;

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: πατέρα τε , καὶ τὸ μητρὸς ἐς σέ μοι ῥέπει
sections.translation: and to you falls the love I should bear my mother , whom I most rightly hate ; and the love I bore my sister , victim of a pitiless sacrifice ; and you were my faithful brother , bringing me your reverence . May Might and Justice ,

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: πάντων μεγίστῳ Ζηνὶ συγγένοιτό σοι . [*]
sections.translation: with Zeus , supreme over all , in the third place , lend you their aid !

sections.label: Section 246
sections.original: Ζεῦ Ζεῦ , θεωρὸς τῶνδε πραγμάτων [*] [*] γενοῦ :
sections.translation: O Zeus , O Zeus , regard our cause ! Behold the orphaned brood of a father eagle that perished in the meshes , in the coils of a fierce viper . They are utterly orphaned , gripped by the famine of hunger :

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: νῆστις πιέζει λιμός : οὐ γὰρ ἐντελεῖς [*]
sections.translation: for they are not grown to full strength to bring their father ’ s quarry to the nest . So you see both me and poor Electra here , children bereft of their father , both outcasts alike from our home .

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: καὶ τοῦ θυτῆρος καί σε τιμῶντος μέγα
sections.translation: If you destroy these nestlings of a father who made sacrifice and revered you greatly , from what like hand will you receive the homage of rich feasts ? Destroy the brood of the eagle and you cannot again send tokens that mortals will trust ;

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: οὔτ ἀρχικός σοι πᾶς ὅδ αὐανθεὶς πυθμὴν
sections.translation: nor , if this royal stock should wither utterly away , will it serve your altars on days when oxen are sacrificed . Oh foster it , and you may raise our house from low estate to great , though now it seems utterly overthrown .

sections.label: Section 264
sections.original: ὦ παῖδες , ὦ σωτῆρες ἑστίας πατρός ,
sections.translation: O children , O saviors of your father ’ s hearth ,

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: σιγᾶθ , ὅπως μὴ πεύσεταί τις , ὦ τέκνα ,
sections.translation: speak not so loud , dear children , in case someone should overhear and report all this to our masters merely for the sake of rumor . May I some day see them dead in the ooze of flaming pitch !

sections.label: Section 269
sections.original: οὔτοι [*] προδώσει Λοξίου μεγασθενὴς
sections.translation: Surely he will not abandon me , the mighty oracle of Loxias ,

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: χρησμὸς κελεύων τόνδε κίνδυνον περᾶν ,
sections.translation: who urged me to brave this peril to the end and loudly proclaims calamities that chill the warmth of my heart , if I do not take vengeance on my father ’ s murderers . He said that ,

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: ἀποχρημάτοισι ζημίαις ταυρούμενον :
sections.translation: I should pay the debt myself with my own life , after many grievous sufferings . For he spoke revealing to mortals the wrath of malignant powers from underneath the earth , and telling of plagues :

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: σαρκῶν ἐπαμβατῆρας ἀγρίαις γνάθοις
sections.translation: leprous ulcers that mount with fierce fangs on the flesh and eat away its primal nature ; and how a white down [*] should sprout up on the diseased place . And he spoke of other assaults of the Furies that are destined to be brought to pass from paternal blood .

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: τὸ γὰρ σκοτεινὸν τῶν ἐνερτέρων βέλος
sections.translation: For the dark bolt of the infernal powers , who are stirred by kindred victims calling for vengeance , and madness , and groundless terrors out of the night , torment and harass a man , and he sees clearly , though he moves his eyebrows in the dark . [*] And with his body marred by the brazen scourge ,

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: χαλκηλάτῳ πλάστιγγι λυμανθὲν δέμας .
sections.translation: he is even chased in exile from his country . And the god declared that to such as these it is not allowed to have a part either in the ceremonial cup or in the cordial libation ; his father ’ s wrath , though unseen , bars him from the altar ; no one receives him or lodges with him ;

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: πάντων δ ἄτιμον κἄφιλον θνῄσκειν χρόνῳ
sections.translation: and at last , despised by all , friendless , he perishes , shrivelled pitifully by a death that wastes him utterly away . Must I not put my trust in oracles such as these ? Yet even if I do not trust them , the deed must still be done . For many impulses conspire to one conclusion .

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: θεοῦ τ ἐφετμαὶ καὶ πατρὸς πένθος μέγα ,
sections.translation: Besides the god ’ s command , my keen grief for my father , and also the pinch of poverty — that my countrymen , the most renowned of mortals , who overthrew Troy in the spirit of glory , should not be subjected so to a pair of women .

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: θήλεια γὰρ φρήν : εἰ δὲ μή , τάχ εἴσεται .
sections.translation: For he has a woman ’ s mind , or if not , it will soon be found out .

sections.label: Section 306
sections.original: ἀλλ ὦ μεγάλαι Μοῖραι , [*] Διόθεν
sections.translation: You mighty Fates , through the power of Zeus grant fulfilment in the way to which Justice now turns . For a word of hate let a word of hate be said ,

sections.label: Section 311
sections.original: πράσσουσα Δίκη μέγ ἀυτεῖ : [*]
sections.translation: Justice cries out as she exacts the debt , and for a murderous stroke let a murderous stroke be paid . Let it be done to him as he does , says the age-old wisdom .

sections.label: Section 1.315
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ αἰνόπατες , [*] τί σοι
sections.translation: O father , unhappy father , by what word or deed of mine can I succeed in sailing from far away to you , where your resting-place holds you , a light to oppose your darkness ?

sections.label: Section 1.320
sections.original: ρον ; [*] χάριτες δ ὁμοίως
sections.translation: Yet a lament in honor of the Atreidae who once possessed our house is none the less a joyous service .

sections.label: Section 2.323
sections.original: τέκνον , φρόνημα τοῦ
sections.translation: My child , the fire ’ s ravening jaw does not overwhelm the wits of the dead man ,

sections.label: Section 2.325
sections.original: πυρὸς [ ἡ [*] ] μαλερὰ γνάθος ,
sections.translation: but afterwards he reveals what stirs him . The murdered man has his dirge ; the guilty man is revealed . Justified lament for fathers and for parents ,

sections.label: Section 2.330
sections.original: γόος ἔνδικος ματεύει
sections.translation: when raised loud and strong , makes its search everywhere .

sections.label: Section 1.332
sections.original: κλῦθὶ νυν , ὦ πάτερ , ἐν μέρει
sections.translation: Hear then , O father , as in turn we mourn with plentiful tears . Look , your two children mourn you

sections.label: Section 1.335
sections.original: θρῆνος ἀναστενάζει .
sections.translation: in a dirge over your tomb . As suppliants and exiles as well they have sought a haven at your sepulchre . What of these things is good , what free of evil ? Is it not hopeless to wrestle against doom ?

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: ἀλλ ἔτ ἂν ἐκ τῶνδε θεὸς χρῄζων
sections.translation: Yet heaven , if it pleases , may still turn our utterance to more joyfully sounding strains . In place of dirges over a tomb , a song of triumph within the royal halls will welcome back a reunited friend . [*]
